remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Partial spontaneous improvement in ~25-30% over 5 years; complete resolution without treatment rare; chronic fluctuating course in majority; central sensitisation often persists
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Multimodal approach (aerobic exercise + CBT + sleep optimisation): most effective combination — 50-60% significant improvement; duloxetine: 50% pain response in 43% vs 29% placebo; milnacipran: similar; pregabalin: 50% pain response 38-50%; low-dose naltrexone (LDN): 30% improvement in open-label studies
remission.gap_size: Large — pharmacotherapy is modestly effective; exercise is the most effective single intervention but chronically underused as 'too painful'; disease is often dismissed
remission.barrier_detail: Fibromyalgia was dismissed as non-organic by mainstream medicine for decades; this has resulted in: delayed diagnosis (mean 5 years), inappropriate investigations, ineffective treatments (opioids in particular), and under-referral to physiotherapy and psychological therapies; the 2010 ACR criteria removed tender point requirement and improved diagnosis
remission.last_soc_change: 2021 — 2021 update to EULAR fibromyalgia recommendations; milnacipran/duloxetine/pregabalin approvals 2007-2008; these remain the only FDA-approved drugs for fibromyalgia; tricyclics (amitriptyline) off-label since 1980s
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term opioid therapy for fibromyalgia (multiple guidelines explicitly contraindicate; worsens central sensitisation and pain thresholds; yet still common — 34% of FM patients on opioids in US); corticosteroids (not indicated; inflammatory basis not proven); muscle relaxants long-term (modest evidence, significant sedation/dependence potential)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Low-dose naltrexone (LDN) 1.5-4.5mg — Phase 2 data showing ~30% pain reduction; mechanism: microglial activation blockade; magnesium malate supplementation (some trial data); S-adenosyl methionine (SAMe) — systematic review positive; transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) — Phase 2 positive; whole-body cryotherapy (emerging evidence)
remission.frontier_signal: Nalmefene (kappa opioid receptor modulation); sodium oxybate (Xyrem for sleep restoration in FM — Phase 3 TONIX failed; sodium oxybate/FMX-8 formulation — Phase 3 REST positive 2023); ketamine IV for refractory FM pain; low-field magnetic stimulation (sTMS); psilocybin for central sensitisation (Phase 2 planned)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
